info.title: Semdragons API
info.description: Agentic workflow coordination modeled as a tabletop RPG. Work items are quests, agents are adventurers, quality reviews are boss battles.

get.description: Returns the current configuration for a specific component
Returns the complete FlowGraph with nodes and edges for all managed components
Returns disconnected nodes and orphaned ports in the component flow

200.description: Component flow gaps and disconnected nodes

get.description: Returns aggregated health status for all managed components
Returns a list of all managed components with basic information
Returns data paths from input components to all reachable components
Returns detailed status for a specific component
Returns array of component metadata including schemas
Returns metadata and schema for a specific component type
Performs FlowGraph connectivity analysis for operational validation (used by E2E tests)

post.description: Deploys a flow to the runtime
Starts a deployed flow
Stops a running flow

get.description: Returns a list of all visual flows

post.description: Creates a new visual flow

get.description: Returns a single flow by ID
Returns health status and timing for flow components (status, uptime, last activity)
Returns filtered message logger entries for flow components (NATS message flow visibility)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return (default: 100, max: 1000)

get.description: Returns runtime metrics for flow components (throughput, errors, queue depth) with graceful degradation

Real-time flow status updates via WebSocket.

## Connection
Connect with: ws://host/flowbuilder/status/stream?flowId={flowId}

## Message Types (Server → Client)
All messages are wrapped in StatusStreamEnvelope:
- **flow_status**: Flow state changes (deployed, running, stopped, failed)
- **component_health**: Component health updates (every 5s)
- **component_metrics**: Real-time metrics from MetricsForwarder
- **log_entry**: Log messages from LogForwarder

## Filtering (Client → Server)
Send SubscribeCommand JSON to filter messages:
- message_types: Array of message types to receive
- log_level: Minimum log level (DEBUG < INFO < WARN < ERROR)
- sources: Array of component names to filter by

## Example Subscribe Command
{"command":"subscribe","message_types":["flow_status","log_entry"],"log_level":"WARN"}

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: Flow ID to subscribe to for status updates

get.description: Returns all registered agents with their current status, level, skills, and stats.

post.description: Recruits a new agent into the game. Agents start at level 1 (Apprentice tier) unless an initial level is specified.

409.description: Agent with this name already exists

get.description: Returns a single agent by ID including level, XP, skills, stats, inventory, and active effects.
Returns active consumable effects for an agent, including remaining duration.
Returns an agent's inventory of owned tools and consumables.

post.description: Uses a consumable item from the agent's inventory, applying its effect.

400.description: Missing consumable_id or item not consumable

post.description: Retires an agent from active duty. No request body required.

get.description: Returns all peer reviews where the agent is either the leader or member. Reviews are blind-masked until completed.

200.description: List of peer reviews involving this agent

get.description: Returns all boss battles (automated review evaluations).
Returns a single boss battle by ID including verdict, scores, and linked quest.

post.description: Pauses autonomous processing. In-progress agentic loops complete gracefully. Manual operations continue. Idempotent — pausing an already-paused board returns the current state.
Resumes autonomous processing. Triggers reconciliation for any quests that transitioned to in_progress while paused. Idempotent.

get.description: Returns the current board play/pause status. When paused, autonomous actions (heartbeats, quest dispatch, boid suggestions) are suspended while manual operations continue.
Returns current token usage counters, hourly limit, budget percentage, and circuit breaker state. The breaker field is 'ok', 'warning' (>=80%), or 'tripped' (>=100%).

post.description: Updates the global hourly token limit. Set to 0 for unlimited. Persisted to KV for restart recovery.
Send a natural language message to the Dungeon Master. Supports two modes: 'converse' (default, Q&A) and 'quest' (create quests/chains with structured output). Sessions persist across turns.

200.description: DM response with optional actions taken

post.description: DM intervenes on an active quest (not yet implemented).

get.description: Returns the conversation history for a DM chat session including all turns with timestamps and trace IDs.

200.description: DM chat session with turn history

post.description: Apply a DM triage decision to a quest in pending_triage status. Body: {path: salvage|tpk|escalate|terminal, analysis: string, salvaged_output?: any, anti_patterns?: string[]}. Salvage preserves partial work and retries. TPK clears output, adds anti-pattern warnings, retries. Escalate marks for human attention. Terminal marks permanently failed.

parameters.name: questId
parameters.description: Quest ID (must be in pending_triage status)

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of entity state changes from the board KV bucket. Sends `event: connected` on connect, then `event: kv_change` for each entity create/update/delete. Includes `initial_sync_complete` operation after all existing entities are sent. Auto-reconnects via SSE `retry` directive.

200.description: SSE stream of KV change events

get.description: Returns all guilds including members, founder, and specialization.
Returns a single guild by ID including members, founder, and specialization.

Returns model registry state. With ?resolve=capability, returns the resolution result for a single capability including endpoint name, model, and provider. Without it, returns a full summary of all endpoints and capabilities.

200.description: Model registry summary or resolution result

get.description: Returns all parties including members, quest assignments, and formation status.
Returns a single party by ID including members, roles, and quest assignment.
Returns all quests with optional filtering by status, difficulty, or guild.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by quest status (posted, claimed, in_progress, in_review, completed, failed)

post.description: Posts a new quest to the quest board. Requires title and goal. Scenarios are optional but recommended — their dependency graph drives automatic party vs solo routing.

400.description: Invalid request body, missing title or goal, or invalid scenario dependencies

post.description: Posts multiple linked quests in a single request. Dependencies use 0-based indices into the quests array, which are resolved to actual quest IDs.

201.description: Array of created quests with resolved dependency IDs

400.description: Invalid request, dependency cycle, or out-of-bounds index

get.description: Returns a single quest by ID.

post.description: Agent abandons a quest, returning it to the board as 'posted'. The claiming agent is released.

200.description: Quest returned to board, agent released

get.description: Downloads all artifact files for a quest as a zip archive. Includes a manifest.json with quest metadata at the root of the archive.
Returns a JSON list of artifact file paths for a quest.
Serves a single artifact file by path within the quest's artifact storage.

post.description: Cancels an in-progress quest by sending a cancel signal to the active agentic loop. The quest transitions to 'failed' and the agent is released. For DAG parent quests, all active sub-quest loops are also cancelled.
Agent claims a posted quest. Validates that the agent meets tier and skill requirements.

403.description: Agent tier too low or missing required skills

409.description: Quest not in 'posted' status or agent not idle

post.description: Marks a quest as completed and releases the claiming agent. No request body required.
Records a quest failure. If attempts remain (< max_attempts), the quest is reposted to the board. Otherwise it is marked as permanently failed. The claiming agent is released in either case.

200.description: Quest failed or reposted, agent released

post.description: Transitions a claimed quest to in_progress. No request body required.
Submits work output for a quest. If the quest requires review (require_review constraint), status transitions to 'in_review'. Otherwise transitions directly to 'completed'.

200.description: Result submitted, status changed to 'in_review' or 'completed'

get.description: Returns all peer reviews with optional filtering. Reviews are blind-masked: partial submissions are hidden until both parties have submitted.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by review status (pending, partial, completed)

post.description: Creates a new peer review for a quest. For party quests, leader and member review each other. For solo quests, set is_solo_task=true and only the leader submits.

400.description: Missing required fields or leader_id == member_id for non-solo review

get.description: Returns a single peer review by ID. Blind-masked: the other party's submission is hidden until the review is completed.

post.description: Submits ratings for a peer review. Each party submits independently. Ratings are integers 1-5. If average rating < 3.0, an explanation is required. When both parties have submitted (or leader for solo), the review is marked completed and average ratings are computed.

400.description: Missing fields, invalid ratings, or missing explanation for low ratings

409.description: Review already completed or party already submitted

get.description: Returns the current runtime configuration including platform identity, NATS status, LLM providers, capabilities, components, workspace, and token budget. API key values are never exposed.

post.description: Mutates runtime-configurable settings (model registry, token budget). Auth required. Platform and NATS settings require a restart. Changes are persisted to disk and propagated via KV.

get.description: Runs live validation checks (NATS, LLM keys, workspace, streams, buckets) and returns an onboarding checklist showing prerequisite status.
Returns available items in the agent store. Optionally personalized by agent level and guild.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Agent ID for personalized catalog (filters by tier eligibility)

post.description: Purchases an item from the store for an agent using XP.

200.description: Purchase result with updated XP balance

get.description: Returns a single store item by ID with pricing, effects, and tier requirements.
Returns the execution trajectory for an agentic loop, including all model calls, tool calls, token usage, and timing. The trajectory ID is the loop_id from a completed quest.
Returns the complete game world state including agents, quests, parties, guilds, battles, and board statistics.
Returns the most recent logged messages from the circular buffer

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 100, max: 10000)

get.description: Query NATS KV bucket entries (development/test only)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 100, max: 1000)

get.description: Stream KV bucket changes via Server-Sent Events (SSE). Supports pattern filtering and SSE reconnection with event IDs.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Key pattern to watch (e.g., 'entity.*'). Default: '*' (all keys)

200.description: SSE stream of KV changes. Events: 'connected' (initial), 'kv_change' (updates), 'error' (failures)

get.description: Returns statistics about processed messages
Returns list of NATS subjects being monitored
Returns all message entries for a specific W3C trace ID, ordered chronologically

paused.description: Whether the board is currently paused

paused_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when board was paused, or null

paused_by.description: Identifier of who paused the board, or null

remove.description: Set true to remove this capability

agent_id.description: ID of the agent claiming the quest

enabled.description: Whether the component is in the config

party_required.description: Whether the quest requires a party
Whether the quest requires a party

is_solo_task.description: Whether this is a solo task review

type.description: Entity type: agent, quest, battle, guild
Entity type: agent, quest, battle, guild

tools_used.description: Tool names used during this turn

api_key_value.description: Actual key value — written to .env, never stored in config

remove.description: Set true to remove this endpoint

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort level (none, low, medium, high)

supports_tools.description: Whether the endpoint supports tool calling

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name for API key

api_key_set.description: Whether the API key env var has a value

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort level (none, low, medium, high)

remove.description: Set true to remove this capability

remove.description: Set true to remove this endpoint

api_key_set.description: Whether the API key env var has a value

model.description: Model identifier at the resolved endpoint

item_id.description: ID of the item to purchase

is_npc.description: Whether this is an NPC agent

level.description: Initial level (1-20, default 1). Higher levels grant higher trust tiers.

enabled.description: Whether the component is in the config

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name for API key

api_key_set.description: Whether the API key env var has a value
Whether an API key is configured

enabled.description: Whether the websocket input is enabled

available.description: Whether the artifact store is available

enabled.description: Enable or disable the websocket input

quest_id.description: Quest to apply the effect to
